Off Calendar Oct-01-2025 Continued To Apr-14-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Justin Hills Cross-Petition For: 1)Confirmation Of Justin Hills' Role As Successor Trutee. 2)Delivery Of All Trust Property To Successor Trustee. 3)Redress Of Breach Of Fiduciary Duty. 4)Return Of Turst Property Pursuant To Probate Code 850. 5)Preliminary And Permanent Injuction Preventing Cross-Respondent Austin Hills And Others From Interfering With The Management Of Dos Colinas Estate. 6)Declaratory Relief Regarding Attorney'S Fees
Off Calendar Oct-01-2025 Continued To Apr-14-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Timothy Chambers, Esq., for Justin Hills, Successor Trustee.
David Brillant, Esq., for Austin Hills, Settlor.

Mr. Brillant stated that he is a trust estate specialist, that he has his own practice, and that he will be making all the appearances for this matter. He also requests a 6-month continuance due to issues with a federal matter regarding this case.

Mr. Chambers is directed to file his objections to Settlor's amended petition by October 15, 2025.

The matters are continued to April 14, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition (1) To Confirm Validity Of The First Amendment; (2) To Compel Accounting And Production Of Trust Records; (3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust And Surcharge; And (4) For An Order Enjoining Respondent From Using Trust Assets
Off Calendar Oct-01-2025 Continued To Apr-14-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition (1) To Confirm Validity Of The First Amendment; (2) To Compel Accounting And Production Of Trust Records; (3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust And Surcharge; And (4) For An Order Enjoining Respondent From Using Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jun-11-2025 Continued To Oct-01-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Timothy Chambers, Esq., for Justin Hills, Successor Trustee.

David Brillant, Esq., for Austin Hills, Settlor.

At the court's query, Mr. Brillant confirms that he has recently submitted a substitution of attorney for efiling and will be counsel for Austin Hills. The preceding is confirmed by the Clerk. There is discussion of whether the parties have considered mediation and regarding when the court might expect a revised objection to the amended petition. Counsel reports of working to resolve this matter and that there is currently no plan to file a revised objection. There is a request to continue this matter for ninety to one hundred and twenty days to allow the parties to continue to meet and confer. The court reminds Mr. Chambers regarding the pending notice issue.

After additional discussion, a continuance date is confirmed and the subject matters are continued to Oct-01-2025 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Petition (1) To Confirm Validity Of The First Amendment; (2) To Compel Accounting And Production Of Trust Records; (3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust And Surcharge; And (4) For An Order Enjoining Respondent From Using Trust Assets
Off Calendar Feb-05-2025 Continued To Jun-11-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Alexandra Banis for Austin Hills, Settlor (via Zoom).
Frank Busch for Justin Hills, Successor Trustee (via Zoom).

Mr. Bush confirmed that objections were filed the previous day. The court inquired about an accounting from Justin Hills. Mr. Bush stated that no formal accounting had been provided yet, but an agreed-upon process is in place.

The parties confirmed mediation occurred in December, and discovery responses from Justin Hills are due early March. Mr. Banas requested a 90-120-day continuance for discovery and potential trial setting. Mr. Bush agreed.

The court continued all three petitions to June 11, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow for completion of discovery and depositions, resolution of service issues, and further mediation efforts.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Off Calendar Jul-22-2024 Continued To Jul-26-24 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 202

NOTICE OF EX PARTE APPLICATION AND EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION SHOULD NOT ISSUE
Off Calendar Jul-22-2024 Denied

Petition (1) To Confirm Validity Of The First Amendment; (2) To Compel Accounting And Production Of Trust Records; (3) For Redress Of Breach Of Trust And Surcharge; And (4) For An Order Enjoining Respondent From Using Trust Assets
Off Calendar Jul-09-2024 Continued To Sep-17-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Alexandra Banis appeared in person for and with Austin Hills by zoom.
Frederick Crombie appeared in person for Justin Hills (respondent).

The parties addressed the issue of venue. The court stated that it will not rule on venue, but that it is keeping the matter here. Mrs. Banis expressed concern about a legal incident that occurred with the respondent, asked for immediate suspension of the successor trustee, spoke of a wedding that is scheduled to occur on the Napa property on July 13th, expressed concern about the successor trustee pocketing the money that will be generated from the wedding and stated that the successor trustee did not obtain permission from Austin to hold the wedding on the property. Mr. Crombie stated that he does not believe Justin will be charged, that the incident does not warrant removal at this time, and that if the court takes any action, that it appoints the next named trustee in line. The court informed the parties that any urgencies that require immediate attention can be requested by way of an ex parte petition. The Petition to Confirm Validity of the First Amendment, etc. is continued to September 17, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Jones. Not reported.
